drill_prompt: In يَسْأَلُكَ, mark the imperfect prefix, verb stem, and object pronoun. Which part contributes 'you'?
learner_explanation: A written Qur'anic token may contain a verb plus an attached object pronoun. The final كَ is not decoration; it contributes 'you' to the hover.
nahw_lesson: The following explicit subject controls contextual wording, but it does not erase the attached object pronoun.
sarf_lesson: Segment the imperfect host and suffix pronoun before choosing an English hover.
what_failed: A lemma/entry gloss was shown as a token hover and the attached object pronoun was omitted.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: and + the sun; and + the moon; and + the stars; and + the mountains; and + the trees
drill_prompt: Break وَالشَّجَرُ into visible pieces and write what each piece contributes to the hover.
learner_explanation: وَالنُّجُومُ is not just 'stars'. The written word contains وَ 'and', ال 'the', and نُّجُومُ 'stars'.
nahw_lesson: The wāw coordinates the noun with the previous phrase; the article remains part of the visible token contribution.
sarf_lesson: For written tokens beginning وَال, segment the conjunction, article, and noun host before glossing.
what_failed: The hover showed host nouns while dropping the visible prefixed conjunction and definite article.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: يَا = O; أَيُّهَا = O you / vocative bridge, with exact token split preserved
drill_prompt: For يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ, label the vocative particle, the vocative bridge, and the addressee.
learner_explanation: يَا supplies the call 'O'. أَيُّهَا helps address the following noun phrase. The hovers should teach the difference.
nahw_lesson: Vocative particles and vocative bridge forms have separate roles in the address construction.
sarf_lesson: Do not merge neighboring function tokens merely because English renders them as one phrase.
visible_bad_hover: O you (who) for both يَا and أَيُّهَا
what_failed: Two adjacent vocative pieces were collapsed into the same phrase gloss, hiding each token's own contribution.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: and + by + the star; by the fig; and/by the olive, preserving the governing particle role
drill_prompt: Compare وَبِالنَّجْمِ and وَالتِّينِ. Which particle creates a prepositional or oath relation?
learner_explanation: In وَبِالنَّجْمِ, the word is not only 'star'. It includes وَ 'and', بِ 'by/with', ال 'the', and the noun host.
nahw_lesson: A bāʾ preposition or oath wāw changes the phrase function; the hover must preserve that relation or stay pending.
sarf_lesson: Attached particles are grammatical pieces of the written token and must be segmented before host glossing.
what_failed: The host noun was translated while the attached preposition or oath particle disappeared.

drill_prompt: Rewrite السُّفَهَاءُ as article plus host without duplicating 'the'.
learner_explanation: السُّفَهَاءُ can be taught as ال 'the' plus سُّفَهَاءُ 'foolish ones', not 'the' plus 'the foolish ones'.
nahw_lesson: The noun remains definite because of the article; English composition should show article plus clean host meaning.
sarf_lesson: When ال is its own segment, the host contribution should not repeat 'the'.
what_failed: The definite article was segmented and then repeated inside the host gloss.
